zh.lifeSummary: 當Sarah Jane Evans於1628年出生在蘇格蘭阿爾迪史爾阿爾迪時，她的父親Hiram Evans41歲，母親Sarah Jane Robinson38歲。她於大約1649年與James H Morrison在蘇格蘭阿爾迪史爾結婚。他們至少是5個兒子的父母。她去世於1736年2月16日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加劍橋大學默里 哈普爾洛金哈姆隆多德利享年108歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sarah Jane Evans于1628年出生在苏格兰阿爾迪史爾阿爾迪时，她的父亲Hiram Evans41岁，母亲Sarah Jane Robinson38岁。她于大约1649年与James H Morrison在苏格兰阿爾迪史爾结婚。他们至少是5个儿子的父母。她去世于1736年2月16日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加劍橋大學默里 哈普爾洛金哈姆隆多德利享年108岁。

nameMeanings.name: Evans
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Welsh: derivative of Evan , from Efan, Ifan, medieval forms of Ieuan (from Latin Johannes, the source of English John), dating from c. 1500, with the post-medieval patronymic suffix -s. Welsh Sion was a separate borrowing of English John, and Evan (Ieuan) and Sion developed as independent names.

variants.name: Evanson
meanings: English (Shropshire and Chesire): patronymic meaning ‘son of Evan’. See Evans .
Americanized form of Norwegian Evensen and of the much less common Swedish cognate Evensson. Compare Eavenson 2 and Evenson 1.
Probably also an Americanized form of Swedish Ivansson: patronymic from the personal name Ivan , of Slavic (Russian) origin.

variants.name: Ivans
meanings: English (Warwickshire and Oxfordshire): variant of   Evans  .
In some cases also an Americanized form of Slovenian Ivanc and also of some other Slavic patronymics from the personal name Ivan .

variants.name: Ivens
meanings: English (Warwickshire): variant of   Evans  .
Flemish, Dutch, and North German: patronymic from the shortened ancient Germanic personal name Ive, Ivo, from Middle High German îwe ‘(bow of) yew’.

variants.name: Avans
meanings: Probably an altered form of Welsh   Evans  .

variants.name: Evens
meanings: Welsh: variant of   Evans  .
German: variant of   Ewens  .

variants.name: Ivins
meanings: English: variant of   Evans  .

variants.name: Evins
meanings: Welsh: variant of   Evans  .

variants.name: Evan
meanings: Welsh (southern): from the personal name Evan, itself from Ieuan, a Welsh derivative of Latin Johannes (English John). See Evans .

nameMeanings.name: Sarah
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Biblical name, borne by the wife of Abraham and mother of Isaac. According to the Book of Genesis, she was originally called Sarai (possibly meaning ‘contentious’ in Hebrew), but had her name changed by God to the more auspicious Sarah ‘princess’ in token of a greater blessing (Genesis 17:15, ‘And God said unto Abraham, As for Sarai thy wife, thou shalt not call her name Sarai, but Sarah shall her name be’). This has been one of the most enduringly popular girls' names.

variants.name: Sara
meanings: Variant of   Sarah .  This is the form used in the Greek of the New Testament (Hebrews 11:11).

nameMeanings.name: Jane
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

variants.name: Jayne
meanings: Elaborated spelling of   Jane  , a form popularized no doubt by the American actress  Jayne Mansfield  (  Vera Jayne Palmer ,  1933–67 ) and British ice-dancer  Jayne Torvill  (  b. 1957 ).

variants.name: Jaine
meanings: Variant spelling of   Jane .

variants.name: Jain
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

historicEvents.title: Massachusetts Colony Gains Control of New Hampshire
historicEvents.description: In 1641, Massachusetts gained control of New Hampshire until 1679 when New Hampshire became a separate royal colony.

historicEvents.title: First Recorded Slave in Portsmouth
historicEvents.description: In 1645, an African American man taken from Guinea was captured and brought to New Hampshire, he would be counted as the first African American slave in New Hampshire.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Black Heritage Trail in New Hampshire- Our African Heritage

en.lifeSummary: When Sarah Jane Evans was born in 1628, in Aberdeen, Aberdeenshire, Scotland, her father, Hiram Evans, was 41 and her mother, Sarah Jane Robinson, was 38. She married James H Morrison about 1649, in Aberdeenshire, Scotland. They were the parents of at least 5 sons. She died on 16 February 1736, in Londonderry, Rockingham, New Hampshire, British Colonial America, at the age of 108.

ko.lifeSummary: Sarah Jane Evans(이)가 1628년 스컷랜드 압르디실르 압르디에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Hiram Evans(은)는 41세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Sarah Jane Robinson(은)는 38세였다. 그녀는 대략 1649년 스컷랜드 압르디실르에서 James H Morrison(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1736년 2월 16일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 뉴 함프실르 록잉함 런도더리에서 108세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sarah Jane Evans родилась в 1628 г. в местности абирдин, абирдиншир, Шотландия, её отцу по имени Hiram Evans было 41 год, а матери, которую звали Sarah Jane Robinson, 38. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени James H Morrison около 1649 г. в местности абирдиншир, Шотландия. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей. Она умерла 16 февраля 1736 г. в местности лондондирри, роккингхам, ню хампшир, бритиш колоняль амирика в возрасте 108 лет.
